bill.constitutionalAuthorityStatementText: From the Congressional Record Online through the Government Publishing Office [www.gpo.gov]By Mr. PETRI:H.R. 4954.Congress has the power to enact this legislation pursuantto the following:Article I, Section 8, Clause 1 of the U.S. Constitutionwhich states: ``The Congress shall have Power To lay andcollect Taxes, Duties, Imposts and Excises, to pay the Debtsand provide for the common Defense and general Welfare of theUnited States; but all Duties, Imposts and Excises shall beuniform throughout the United States;''ANDArticle I, Section 8, Clause 3 of the U.S. Constitutionwhich grants Congress the power ``To regulate Commerce withforeign Nations, and among the several States, and with theIndian Tribes;''[Page H2211]

item.text: Referred to the Subcommittee on Trade.
Referred to the House Committee on Ways and Means.

summary.text: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on certain compression-ignition internal combustion piston engines.

bill.title: To suspend temporarily the duty on certain compression-ignition internal combustion piston engines.

item.title: To suspend temporarily the duty on certain compression-ignition internal combustion piston engines.
To suspend temporarily the duty on certain compression-ignition internal combustion piston engines.

latestAction.text: Referred to the Subcommittee on Trade.

dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.
dublinCore.dc:contributor: Congressional Research Service, Library of Congress
dublinCore.dc:description: This file contains bill summaries and statuses for federal legislation. A bill summary describes the most significant provisions of a piece of legislation and details the effects the legislative text may have on current law and federal programs. Bill summaries are authored by the Congressional Research Service (CRS) of the Library of Congress. As stated in Public Law 91-510 (2 USC 166 (d)(6)), one of the duties of CRS is "to prepare summaries and digests of bills and resolutions of a public general nature introduced in the Senate or House of Representatives". For more information, refer to the User Guide that accompanies this file.
